nodes.standard_name: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
nodes.original_text_name: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
nodes.context: Far-right survivalist anti-government militia advocating Christian Identity, active in the US in the 1970s-1980s.
Baptist congregation founded in 1971 in Pontiac, Missouri, which evolved into the CSA.
A community founded by Robert G. Millar, where James Ellison moved after prison.
White supremacist organization based in Hayden Lake, Idaho, led by Richard Girnt Butler.

nodes.standard_name: Mountain Church of Jesus Christ the Savior
nodes.original_text_name: Mountain Church of Jesus Christ the Savior
nodes.context: Extreme right-wing church founded by Robert E. Miles in Cohoctah, Michigan.
White supremacist hate group with which Ellison had close ties.
White supremacist revolutionary group formed in Idaho, which some CSA members joined.
White supremacist prison gang with links to the CSA.
Outlaw motorcycle club that provided training to the CSA in exchange for weapons.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: CSA developed from Zarephath-Horeb Community Church.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: Ellison was mentored by Butler, founder of Aryan Nations, and had close ties to the group.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.target: Mountain Church of Jesus Christ the Savior
edges.context: Ellison was mentored by Miles, founder of the Mountain Church, and the groups had links.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: Ellison had close ties to the Ku Klux Klan.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: CSA had links to The Order; some CSA members joined The Order.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: CSA had links to the Aryan Brotherhood.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: Pagans provided training to CSA in exchange for weapons.

edges.target: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: The Order was formed by former CSA members after a rift.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: CSA founder James Ellison moved to Elohim City after prison and married Millar's granddaughter.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
edges.context: CSA member Leonard Ginter hosted a meeting that led to the death of Posse Comitatus member Gordon Kahl.
